subject: Project Management
title: Jidoka: Built-In Quality
description: A 60-second lesson on Jidoka: Built-In Quality.

hook.text: At Toyota, any worker can stop the entire production line if they spot a defect. It's called jidoka.

buildup.text: The concept came from Sakichi Toyoda's automatic loom, which stopped itself when a thread broke.

discovery.text: Jidoka means 'automation with a human touch' — machines detect problems, humans solve them.

twist.text: Stopping the line costs money short-term. But it prevents defective products from reaching customers.

climax.text: Jidoka builds quality into the process instead of inspecting it afterward — prevention over detection.

punchline.text: Stop the line. Fix the problem. Never pass a defect along.

quiz.question: What does jidoka mean?
options: Automation with a human touch — stopping production to fix defects
